authors.name: Jane Austen
authors.biography: English novelist whose six major novels critique the British landed gentry with irony and social insight. Among the most widely read writers in English literature.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.description: Elizabeth Bennet spars with the proud Mr. Darcy in a sparkling comedy of manners about marriage, money, and first impressions in Regency England.

books.title: Sense and Sensibility
books.description: Sisters Elinor and Marianne Dashwood navigate love and heartbreak after their family's fortune is lost, embodying reason and emotion in turn.

books.title: Emma
books.description: A clever, wealthy young woman amuses herself with matchmaking in her small village, blind to her own heart until her schemes unravel.

books.title: Persuasion
books.description: Anne Elliot is given a second chance at love when Captain Wentworth, the suitor she was persuaded to refuse years earlier, returns from sea.

authors.name: Charles Dickens
authors.biography: Victorian novelist and social critic who created some of fiction's best-known characters. His serialized novels exposed the harsh conditions of industrial England.

books.title: Great Expectations
books.description: Orphan Pip rises from the Kent marshes to London society on a mysterious benefactor's money, learning hard lessons about class, loyalty, and gratitude.

books.title: A Tale of Two Cities
books.description: Love and sacrifice bind London and Paris during the French Revolution, culminating in one of literature's most famous acts of redemption.

books.title: Oliver Twist
books.description: A workhouse orphan falls in with a gang of juvenile pickpockets in the London underworld in this dark portrait of Victorian poverty and crime.

books.title: David Copperfield
books.description: Dickens's most autobiographical novel follows David from a blighted childhood through hardship, friendship, and love to maturity as a writer.

authors.name: Charlotte Brontë
authors.biography: Eldest of the three Brontë sisters, whose passionate, psychologically rich novels challenged Victorian conventions about women's inner lives.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.description: A plain, fiercely principled governess falls in love with her brooding employer Mr. Rochester, only to discover the terrible secret hidden in Thornfield Hall.

authors.name: Emily Brontë
authors.biography: English novelist and poet who published a single novel of elemental passion before dying at thirty. Wrote under the pen name Ellis Bell.

books.title: Wuthering Heights
books.description: The obsessive, destructive love between Catherine Earnshaw and the foundling Heathcliff plays out across two generations on the wild Yorkshire moors.

authors.name: Leo Tolstoy
authors.biography: Russian master regarded as one of the greatest novelists of all time, whose epic realism encompasses war, family, faith, and the search for meaning.

books.title: Anna Karenina
books.description: A married aristocrat's affair with Count Vronsky scandalizes St. Petersburg society, set against Levin's parallel search for an honest life on the land.

books.title: War and Peace
books.description: Five aristocratic families live, love, and suffer through Napoleon's invasion of Russia in this vast meditation on history, free will, and fate.

books.title: The Death of Ivan Ilyich
books.description: A successful judge confronts the emptiness of his conventional life as he faces a slow, terrifying death in this piercing novella.

authors.name: Fyodor Dostoevsky
authors.biography: Russian novelist and philosopher whose psychological depth and moral urgency shaped modern literature. Survived a mock execution and Siberian exile.

books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.description: Impoverished student Raskolnikov murders a pawnbroker to prove a theory about extraordinary men, then unravels under guilt and the patient pursuit of a detective.

books.title: The Brothers Karamazov
books.description: A patricide forces three brothers — sensualist, intellectual, and monk — to confront faith, doubt, and responsibility in Dostoevsky's final masterpiece.

books.title: Notes from Underground
books.description: A bitter, hyperconscious retired clerk rails against rationalism and his own spite in the monologue that anticipated existentialism.

books.title: The Idiot
books.description: The saintly Prince Myshkin returns to Russia and is destroyed by a society that cannot accommodate genuine goodness.

authors.name: Mark Twain
authors.biography: American humorist, riverboat pilot, and novelist called the father of American literature, famed for his satire and vernacular style.

books.title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
books.description: Huck and the runaway slave Jim raft down the Mississippi in a picaresque masterpiece that skewers the hypocrisies of antebellum America.

books.title: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer
books.description: A mischievous boy in a Missouri river town whitewashes fences, hunts treasure, and witnesses a murder in this classic of American boyhood.

authors.name: F. Scott Fitzgerald
authors.biography: American novelist and chronicler of the Jazz Age whose glittering prose captured the promise and corruption of the American Dream.

books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.description: Mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby throws lavish parties across the bay from Daisy Buchanan, the lost love he is determined to win back, in the definitive Jazz Age novel.

books.title: Tender Is the Night
books.description: Glamorous psychiatrist Dick Diver and his wife Nicole disintegrate on the French Riviera in Fitzgerald's most personal novel.

books.title: This Side of Paradise
books.description: Princeton golden boy Amory Blaine drifts through romance and disillusionment in the debut that made Fitzgerald famous at twenty-three.

authors.name: Ernest Hemingway
authors.biography: Nobel Prize-winning American writer whose spare, understated style revolutionized twentieth-century prose. Ambulance driver, war correspondent, and big-game fisherman.

books.title: The Old Man and the Sea
books.description: An aging Cuban fisherman battles a giant marlin far out in the Gulf Stream in a taut parable of endurance and dignity in defeat.

books.title: A Farewell to Arms
books.description: An American ambulance officer and an English nurse fall in love amid the chaos of the Italian front in World War I.

books.title: The Sun Also Rises
books.description: Expatriate Jake Barnes and the magnetic Lady Brett Ashley drift from Paris cafés to the bullfights of Pamplona in the novel that defined the Lost Generation.

books.title: For Whom the Bell Tolls
books.description: American dynamiter Robert Jordan joins Republican guerrillas in the Spanish Civil War for three days that hold a lifetime of love and sacrifice.

authors.name: John Steinbeck
authors.biography: Nobel laureate whose novels of Depression-era California gave voice to migrant workers and the dispossessed with compassion and biblical sweep.

books.title: The Grapes of Wrath
books.description: The Joad family flees the Oklahoma Dust Bowl for California's promised land, finding exploitation and solidarity on the road west.

books.title: Of Mice and Men
books.description: Drifting ranch hands George and Lennie nurse a dream of owning land in a tragedy of friendship and broken hopes.

books.title: East of Eden
books.description: Two families reenact Cain and Abel across generations in California's Salinas Valley in Steinbeck's sprawling meditation on choice and inherited sin.

books.title: Cannery Row
books.description: Mack and the boys scheme to throw a party for Doc the marine biologist in this affectionate portrait of a Monterey waterfront community.

authors.name: Virginia Woolf
authors.biography: Modernist pioneer whose stream-of-consciousness novels mapped the texture of inner life. Central figure of the Bloomsbury Group and feminist criticism.

books.title: Mrs Dalloway
books.description: On a single June day in London, Clarissa Dalloway prepares for a party while shell-shocked veteran Septimus Smith spirals, their lives glancingly entwined.

books.title: To the Lighthouse
books.description: The Ramsay family's summers on the Isle of Skye frame a luminous elegy for time, art, and the unsaid spaces between people.

books.title: Orlando
books.description: An Elizabethan nobleman lives four centuries and changes sex along the way in Woolf's playful, gender-bending mock biography.

authors.name: James Joyce
authors.biography: Irish modernist whose linguistic invention and encyclopedic ambition redefined what the novel could do.

books.title: Ulysses
books.description: Leopold Bloom wanders Dublin on June 16, 1904, in a single ordinary day rendered with Homeric scope and dazzling stylistic invention.

books.title: Dubliners
books.description: Fifteen exacting stories of paralysis and epiphany among the ordinary citizens of turn-of-the-century Dublin.

authors.name: Franz Kafka
authors.biography: Prague insurance clerk who wrote nightmarish fables of bureaucracy, guilt, and alienation that gave the twentieth century its defining adjective: Kafkaesque.

books.title: The Metamorphosis
books.description: Traveling salesman Gregor Samsa wakes one morning transformed into a monstrous insect, and his family's love curdles into shame.

books.title: The Trial
books.description: Josef K. is arrested by an inaccessible court for a crime no one will name, and his search for justice becomes a descent into absurdity.

books.title: The Castle
books.description: Land surveyor K. struggles endlessly to reach the authorities of a castle that governs a village in Kafka's unfinished parable of unreachable power.

authors.name: Gabriel García Márquez
authors.biography: Colombian Nobel laureate and master of magical realism, whose imagined town of Macondo became a mirror for Latin America's history.

books.title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
books.description: Seven generations of the Buendía family rise and fall with the mythical town of Macondo in the landmark novel of magical realism.

books.title: Love in the Time of Cholera
books.description: Florentino Ariza waits more than fifty years for Fermina Daza in a lush meditation on love's persistence in every form.

books.title: Chronicle of a Death Foretold
books.description: Everyone in town knows the Vicario brothers intend to kill Santiago Nasar, yet no one stops them, in this compact anatomy of honor and complicity.

authors.name: Jorge Luis Borges
authors.biography: Argentine poet, essayist, and writer of metaphysical short fictions about labyrinths, mirrors, infinite libraries, and the nature of time.

books.title: Ficciones
books.description: Seventeen dazzling stories — infinite libraries, forking paths, imaginary encyclopedias — that fold philosophy into fiction like origami.

books.title: The Aleph
books.description: Stories of points containing all points, immortals weary of immortality, and memory beyond bearing, from literature's great miniaturist.

authors.name: Isabel Allende
authors.biography: Chilean-American novelist whose sweeping family sagas blend the political and the magical. One of the most widely read Spanish-language authors alive.

books.title: The House of the Spirits
books.description: Three generations of Trueba women — clairvoyant Clara, ethereal Blanca, revolutionary Alba — endure love and tyranny in an unnamed Latin American country.

books.title: Eva Luna
books.description: An orphaned storyteller spins her way from servitude to self-invention against a backdrop of revolution and telenovela passion.

authors.name: Italo Calvino
authors.biography: Italian fabulist whose playful, crystalline fictions range from neorealism to postmodern experiment, beloved for their lightness and invention.

books.title: Invisible Cities
books.description: Marco Polo describes fifty-five impossible cities to an aging Kublai Khan in a jeweled meditation on imagination, memory, and desire.

books.title: If on a winter's night a traveler
books.description: You, the Reader, begin ten different novels that keep interrupting each other in Calvino's witty labyrinth about the act of reading itself.

authors.name: Umberto Eco
authors.biography: Italian semiotician and novelist who smuggled medieval philosophy, conspiracy, and bibliophilia into international bestsellers.

books.title: The Name of the Rose
books.description: Franciscan friar William of Baskerville investigates a series of murders in a fourteenth-century abbey whose labyrinthine library guards a deadly secret.

books.title: Foucault's Pendulum
books.description: Three Milanese editors invent a grand occult conspiracy as a game, until believers decide the Plan is real and want what they know.

authors.name: Albert Camus
authors.biography: French-Algerian novelist and philosopher of the absurd, Nobel laureate at forty-four, who insisted on lucidity and revolt in a meaningless universe.

books.title: The Stranger
books.description: Indifferent clerk Meursault kills an Arab man on a sun-blinded Algerian beach and is condemned as much for failing to weep at his mother's funeral.

books.title: The Plague
books.description: As plague seals the city of Oran, Dr. Rieux and an ensemble of citizens choose decency over despair in an allegory of occupation and solidarity.

books.title: The Fall
books.description: In an Amsterdam bar, former Parisian lawyer Clamence delivers a seductive confession that indicts himself — and his listener.

authors.name: Victor Hugo
authors.biography: Towering French Romantic — poet, dramatist, novelist, and exile — whose epic novels championed the poor and the outcast.

books.title: Les Misérables
books.description: Ex-convict Jean Valjean seeks redemption across decades of French upheaval while the implacable Inspector Javert hunts him.

books.title: The Hunchback of Notre-Dame
books.description: The deformed bell-ringer Quasimodo, the dancer Esmeralda, and the tormented archdeacon Frollo collide beneath the towers of medieval Paris.

authors.name: Alexandre Dumas
authors.biography: Prolific French storyteller whose swashbuckling historical adventures remain among the most entertaining novels ever written.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.description: Falsely imprisoned sailor Edmond Dantès escapes, finds a fabulous treasure, and executes an intricate revenge on the men who betrayed him.

books.title: The Three Musketeers
books.description: Hot-headed young d'Artagnan joins Athos, Porthos, and Aramis in court intrigue, duels, and derring-do in seventeenth-century France.

authors.name: Vladimir Nabokov
authors.biography: Russian-American stylist and lepidopterist whose virtuosic English prose made him one of the twentieth century's most acclaimed and controversial novelists.

books.title: Lolita
books.description: Unreliable aesthete Humbert Humbert narrates his obsession with twelve-year-old Dolores Haze in a morally harrowing, linguistically dazzling novel.

books.title: Pale Fire
books.description: A 999-line poem and its deranged scholarly commentary tell two stories at once in Nabokov's funniest, most intricate puzzle box.

authors.name: George Orwell
authors.biography: English essayist and novelist whose clear-eyed hatred of totalitarianism and cant produced the century's most influential political fiction.

books.description: Winston Smith rewrites history for the Ministry of Truth and dares to fall in love under Big Brother's all-seeing eye in the defining dystopia of surveillance and thought control.

books.title: Animal Farm
books.description: The animals of Manor Farm overthrow their farmer only to watch the pigs become indistinguishable from the masters, in a fable of revolution betrayed.

books.title: Homage to Catalonia
books.description: Orwell's firsthand account of fighting with a POUM militia in the Spanish Civil War, and of the revolution devoured by its own side.

authors.name: Aldous Huxley
authors.biography: English novelist and essayist from a famous scientific family, who imagined a future enslaved not by pain but by pleasure.

books.title: Brave New World
books.description: In a World State of engineered castes, soma, and manufactured happiness, a 'savage' raised on Shakespeare collides with civilization.

books.title: The Doors of Perception
books.description: Huxley's famous account of his mescaline experiment, a short, lucid inquiry into perception, art, and the mind at large.

authors.name: Toni Morrison
authors.biography: Nobel laureate whose novels gave epic, lyric form to Black American memory and survival. The first African American woman to win the Nobel Prize in Literature.

books.title: Beloved
books.description: Escaped slave Sethe is haunted — literally — by the daughter she killed to spare from bondage, in a ghost story about slavery's unhealed wounds.

books.title: Song of Solomon
books.description: Macon 'Milkman' Dead journeys south through family legend toward flight and self-discovery in Morrison's mythic coming-of-age novel.

books.title: The Bluest Eye
books.description: Young Pecola Breedlove prays for blue eyes in a devastating debut about beauty standards, racism, and a child the world refuses to love.

books.title: Sula
books.description: Two girls grow into very different women in the Black hilltop community of Medallion, Ohio, in a fierce novel of friendship and betrayal.

authors.name: James Baldwin
authors.biography: American novelist and essayist whose prophetic eloquence on race, sexuality, and the American conscience remains urgently contemporary.

books.title: Go Tell It on the Mountain
books.description: On his fourteenth birthday, John Grimes wrestles with faith, family, and his stepfather's church in Baldwin's semi-autobiographical Harlem debut.

books.title: Giovanni's Room
books.description: An American in Paris falls in love with an Italian bartender and destroys them both through shame, in a landmark of queer literature.

books.title: If Beale Street Could Talk
books.description: Nineteen-year-old Tish fights to free her wrongly imprisoned fiancé Fonny as their families rally, in a love story under the weight of injustice.

authors.name: Zora Neale Hurston
authors.biography: Anthropologist and central figure of the Harlem Renaissance who captured Black Southern folk life in luminous vernacular prose.

books.title: Their Eyes Were Watching God
books.description: Janie Crawford moves through three marriages toward self-possession and the horizon in a classic of Black womanhood and Florida folk life.

authors.name: Ralph Ellison
authors.biography: American writer whose single completed novel stands among the great works of the century. Also a penetrating essayist on jazz and democracy.

books.title: Invisible Man
books.description: An unnamed Black man journeys from a Southern college to Harlem's underground, rendered invisible by everyone's refusal to see him.

authors.name: Kurt Vonnegut
authors.biography: American satirist and humanist who survived the firebombing of Dresden as a POW and turned dark history into darkly funny, deeply moral fiction.

books.title: Slaughterhouse-Five
books.description: Billy Pilgrim comes unstuck in time, shuttling between Dresden's firebombing and the planet Tralfamadore. So it goes.

books.title: Cat's Cradle
books.description: A journalist chasing the atomic bomb's legacy finds ice-nine, a doomsday substance, and Bokononism, a religion of comforting lies.

books.title: Breakfast of Champions
books.description: Failing car dealer Dwayne Hoover meets pulp writer Kilgore Trout, whose novel convinces him everyone else is a robot, in Vonnegut's anarchic satire of America.

authors.name: Joseph Heller
authors.biography: American novelist and WWII bombardier whose first novel added a phrase to the language for absurd, self-defeating logic.

books.title: Catch-22
books.description: Bombardier Yossarian schemes to stay alive against a military bureaucracy whose rules make escape logically impossible, in the great absurdist war novel.

authors.name: Sylvia Plath
authors.biography: American poet of ferocious precision whose confessional verse and single novel made her an enduring literary icon.

books.title: The Bell Jar
books.description: Brilliant college student Esther Greenwood descends into depression during a New York magazine internship in this acid, semi-autobiographical classic.

authors.name: Chinua Achebe
authors.biography: Nigerian novelist called the father of modern African literature, who told colonialism's story from the other side of the river.

books.title: Things Fall Apart
books.description: Proud wrestler and yam farmer Okonkwo watches Igbo society and his own life shatter against the arrival of missionaries and colonial rule.

books.title: No Longer at Ease
books.description: Okonkwo's grandson, educated in England, returns to a civil service post in Lagos and is ground down between two value systems.

authors.name: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
authors.biography: Nigerian novelist and essayist whose fiction spans the Biafran War to Black immigrant life in America, and whose talks reshaped popular feminism.

books.title: Half of a Yellow Sun
books.description: Twin sisters, a houseboy, and an English writer are swept into the Biafran War in a wrenching novel of love and the Nigerian civil war.

books.title: Americanah
books.description: Ifemelu leaves Lagos for America and a blogging career about race, while her first love Obinze navigates undocumented life in London.

books.title: Purple Hibiscus
books.description: Fifteen-year-old Kambili discovers freedom at her aunt's house, away from her wealthy father's pious tyranny, as Nigeria convulses with a coup.

authors.name: J.M. Coetzee
authors.biography: South African-born Nobel laureate whose austere, unsparing novels probe complicity, power, and the limits of sympathy.

books.title: Disgrace
books.description: A disgraced Cape Town professor retreats to his daughter's farm, where a brutal attack forces both to reckon with post-apartheid South Africa.

books.title: Waiting for the Barbarians
books.description: A frontier magistrate's conscience awakens as the Empire he serves manufactures a war against the 'barbarians' beyond the wall.

authors.name: Salman Rushdie
authors.biography: Indian-born British novelist whose exuberant magical realism remade the postcolonial novel, and whose life became a global referendum on free expression.

books.title: Midnight's Children
books.description: Saleem Sinai, born at the exact moment of India's independence, is telepathically linked to a thousand other midnight children in Rushdie's Booker-of-Bookers epic.

books.title: The Satanic Verses
books.description: Two Indian actors fall from an exploding airliner and assume angelic and demonic aspects in the phantasmagoric novel that provoked a fatwa.

authors.name: Arundhati Roy
authors.biography: Indian novelist and essayist whose Booker-winning debut took the world by storm; a fearless critic of power in her nonfiction.

books.title: The God of Small Things
books.description: Twins Rahel and Estha circle the forbidden love and small cruelties that destroyed their family in 1960s Kerala, in prose of startling invention.

authors.name: Jhumpa Lahiri
authors.biography: Pulitzer-winning American author of Bengali heritage whose precise, quiet fiction maps immigrant longing across generations and continents.

books.title: Interpreter of Maladies
books.description: Nine stories of Indians and Indian Americans suspended between homelands, including the Pulitzer-winning title story of a tour guide's brief intimacy.

books.title: The Namesake
books.description: Gogol Ganguli spends a lifetime negotiating the name his Bengali parents gave him and the American life he wants, in a tender novel of two generations.

authors.name: Haruki Murakami
authors.biography: Japanese novelist of jazz bars, missing cats, parallel worlds, and profound loneliness — among the most widely read literary authors on earth.

books.title: Norwegian Wood
books.description: College student Toru Watanabe is torn between fragile, beautiful Naoko and vivacious Midori in the nostalgic love story that made Murakami famous in Japan.

books.title: Kafka on the Shore
books.description: A runaway boy named Kafka and an old man who talks to cats follow converging fates through a Japan where fish rain from the sky.

books.title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
books.description: Unemployed Toru Okada searches for his missing wife down Tokyo alleys and dry wells, uncovering wartime horrors and psychic battles.

books.title: 1Q84
books.description: An assassin and a math teacher slip into a world with two moons where a cult's Little People pull threads, in Murakami's gigantic parallel-reality love story.

authors.name: Yasunari Kawabata
authors.biography: First Japanese winner of the Nobel Prize in Literature, whose spare, melancholy novels distill beauty from transience.

books.title: Snow Country
books.description: A wealthy Tokyo dilettante and a provincial geisha conduct a doomed affair at a remote hot-spring town in Japan's snowbound west.

authors.name: Kazuo Ishiguro
authors.biography: Japanese-born British Nobel laureate whose deceptively quiet narrators reveal, between the lines, the griefs they cannot face.

books.title: The Remains of the Day
books.description: Aging butler Stevens motors through the English countryside reckoning with decades of perfect service to a Nazi-sympathizing lord, and a love he let pass.

books.title: Never Let Me Go
books.description: Kathy, Ruth, and Tommy grow up at an idyllic boarding school whose students slowly learn what their bodies are for.

books.title: Klara and the Sun
books.description: Solar-powered Artificial Friend Klara observes the humans she serves with devoted, alien clarity, hoping to save the sick girl she loves.

books.title: An Artist of the Floating World
books.description: A retired painter who once made propaganda for imperial Japan negotiates his daughters' marriages and his own selective memory in postwar Nagasaki.

authors.name: Ian McEwan
authors.biography: English novelist of moral hinge-points, where a single act or misreading detonates across decades.

books.title: Atonement
books.description: Thirteen-year-old Briony's false accusation destroys two lives, and her lifetime of atonement becomes the novel itself.

books.title: On Chesil Beach
books.description: A wedding night in 1962 goes catastrophically wrong for two virgins who love each other but cannot speak, in a novella of devastating restraint.

authors.name: Margaret Atwood
authors.biography: Canadian poet and novelist whose speculative fictions about power over women's bodies proved uncomfortably predictive.

books.title: The Handmaid's Tale
books.description: In the theocratic Republic of Gilead, Offred is a Handmaid valued only for her womb, narrating survival under a regime built on women's subjugation.

books.title: The Testaments
books.description: Fifteen years after Offred's story, three women — including the fearsome Aunt Lydia — narrate Gilead's rot from within.

books.title: Alias Grace
books.description: Convicted murderess Grace Marks tells her story to a young doctor in 1850s Canada, and the truth stays just out of reach.

books.title: Oryx and Crake
books.description: Snowman, perhaps the last human, remembers his friend Crake and the gene-spliced apocalypse that ended the world in Atwood's biotech dystopia.

authors.name: Alice Munro
authors.biography: Canadian Nobel laureate called the master of the contemporary short story, who fit whole lifetimes into thirty pages of small-town Ontario.

books.title: Dear Life
books.description: Fourteen stories of swerves and reckonings in ordinary Canadian lives, closing with four autobiographical pieces Munro called her final words on her own life.

authors.name: Donna Tartt
authors.biography: American novelist who publishes roughly once a decade to enormous acclaim, blending literary density with propulsive plotting.

books.title: The Secret History
books.description: Six classics students at an elite Vermont college murder one of their own, and the narrator explains exactly how it came to that, in the original dark-academia novel.

books.title: The Goldfinch
books.description: A boy survives a museum bombing that kills his mother and walks out with a priceless Dutch painting that anchors and warps his life.

authors.name: Anthony Doerr
authors.biography: American novelist whose intricately structured books braid science, history, and wonder. Won the Pulitzer Prize for fiction in 2015.

books.title: All the Light We Cannot See
books.description: A blind French girl and a German radio prodigy converge on occupied Saint-Malo in 1944, in a Pulitzer-winning novel of light in dark times.

books.title: Cloud Cuckoo Land
books.description: An ancient Greek tale links five dreamers across six centuries — from the fall of Constantinople to a generation starship — in an ode to libraries and stories.

authors.name: Colson Whitehead
authors.biography: American novelist and two-time Pulitzer winner who reinvents himself with each book, from speculative history to crime caper.

books.title: The Underground Railroad
books.description: Cora flees a Georgia plantation on a literal underground railroad, each state a different experiment in American cruelty, in Whitehead's Pulitzer-winning alternate history.

books.title: The Nickel Boys
books.description: Two Black boys endure a brutal Florida reform school in the Jim Crow era, based on the real Dozier School, in a novel of crushing moral force.

authors.name: Emily St. John Mandel
authors.biography: Canadian novelist whose elegant speculative fictions about collapse and connection became unexpected comfort reading in actual catastrophe.

books.title: Station Eleven
books.description: A traveling Shakespeare troupe crosses the Great Lakes region twenty years after a flu pandemic ends civilization, because survival is insufficient.

books.title: Sea of Tranquility
books.description: A glitch in time links a remittance man in 1912, a novelist on a pandemic book tour, and a moon-colony detective in a tale of simulation and mercy.

books.title: The Glass Hotel
books.description: A Ponzi scheme's collapse ripples through the lives of a bartender, her brother, and a financier's 'kingdom of money' in a ghostly novel of counterlives.

authors.name: Ann Patchett
authors.biography: American novelist and bookstore owner whose warm, precise fiction explores improvised families and the long half-life of choices.

books.title: Bel Canto
books.description: Terrorists seize a birthday party at a South American vice-presidential mansion, and over months of siege, captors and hostages fall into music and love.

books.title: The Dutch House
books.description: Siblings Danny and Maeve orbit the gaudy mansion their stepmother expelled them from, across five decades of devotion and grievance.

authors.name: Elizabeth Strout
authors.biography: Pulitzer-winning American novelist of small-town Maine interiority, whose linked stories accumulate into devastating portraits.

books.title: Olive Kitteridge
books.description: Thirteen linked stories orbit a blunt, difficult retired math teacher in coastal Maine, in a Pulitzer-winning mosaic of ordinary grief and grace.

books.title: My Name Is Lucy Barton
books.description: From a hospital bed, a writer talks with the estranged mother at her bedside, circling the poverty and damage of her childhood.

authors.name: Amor Towles
authors.biography: American investment banker turned novelist, beloved for urbane, beautifully carpentered historical fiction.

books.title: A Gentleman in Moscow
books.description: Count Alexander Rostov is sentenced to house arrest in the Metropol Hotel in 1922 and builds a rich, full life inside its walls across three Soviet decades.

books.title: Rules of Civility
books.description: A chance meeting in a Greenwich Village jazz bar on New Year's Eve 1937 vaults secretary Katey Kontent through Manhattan's glittering upper circles.

books.title: The Lincoln Highway
books.description: In 1954, four boys set out from Nebraska for California but are hijacked — charmingly, disastrously — toward New York, over ten propulsive days.

authors.name: Gabrielle Zevin
authors.biography: American novelist and screenwriter whose breakout book made the creative partnership its great love story.

books.title: Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow
books.description: Sam and Sadie build video games and a thirty-year creative partnership marked by genius, betrayal, and the play that makes life bearable.

books.title: The Storied Life of A.J. Fikry
books.description: A grumpy widowed bookseller on a small island finds a toddler abandoned in his shop, and his curmudgeonly life reshelves itself around her.

authors.name: Fredrik Backman
authors.biography: Swedish blogger turned global phenomenon, who writes comedies that ambush readers into weeping about community and grief.

books.title: A Man Called Ove
books.description: A cantankerous widower's meticulously planned suicide keeps getting interrupted by pregnant neighbors, stray cats, and an entire community that needs him.

books.title: Anxious People
books.description: A failed bank robber takes an apartment viewing hostage, and everyone turns out to be the worst — and most forgivable — version of themselves.

books.title: Beartown
books.description: A fading forest town pins everything on its junior hockey team until a violent act splits the community along lines of loyalty and truth.

authors.name: Sally Rooney
authors.biography: Irish novelist whose flat, exact prose about millennial intimacy and class made her a generational touchstone.

books.title: Normal People
books.description: Connell and Marianne fall in and out of each other's lives from small-town Sligo to Trinity College in an anatomy of class, damage, and love.

books.title: Conversations with Friends
books.description: Student poet Frances begins an affair with a married actor while her best friend and ex, Bobbi, watches it all unfold in cool, witty detachment.

authors.name: Taylor Jenkins Reid
authors.biography: American novelist of glamorous, emotionally savvy fictional oral histories of fame from old Hollywood to seventies rock.

books.title: The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
books.description: Reclusive Hollywood legend Evelyn Hugo finally tells the truth about her seven marriages — and the one great love she could never name — to an unknown journalist.

books.title: Daisy Jones & The Six
books.description: An oral history of a seventies rock band's meteoric rise and mid-tour implosion, and of the combustible chemistry between its two front people.

books.title: Malibu Rising
books.description: Over one August day and night in 1983, the four famous Riva siblings throw the party that burns their family's secrets — and house — to the ground.

authors.name: Celeste Ng
authors.biography: American novelist whose bestsellers dissect family secrets, race, and motherhood in meticulously observed communities.

books.title: Little Fires Everywhere
books.description: An artist and her daughter upend the planned perfection of Shaker Heights, Ohio, igniting a custody battle and the Richardson family itself.

books.title: Everything I Never Told You
books.description: Lydia Lee, the favorite child of a Chinese American family in 1970s Ohio, is found drowned, and her family's carefully unspoken hopes surface.

authors.name: Khaled Hosseini
authors.biography: Afghan-American physician turned novelist whose books brought Afghanistan's wars and griefs to tens of millions of readers.

books.title: The Kite Runner
books.description: Wealthy Amir betrays his loyal friend Hassan in 1970s Kabul and spends decades seeking a way to be good again.

books.title: A Thousand Splendid Suns
books.description: Mariam and Laila, married to the same brutal man, forge an unbreakable bond through thirty years of Afghan war and loss.

authors.name: Min Jin Lee
authors.biography: Korean American novelist whose immersive sagas chronicle diaspora, dignity, and the cost of belonging.

books.title: Pachinko
books.description: Four generations of a Korean family endure exile and discrimination in twentieth-century Japan, beginning with Sunja's fateful pregnancy in a fishing village.

authors.name: Madeline Miller
authors.biography: American classicist who retells Greek myth with scholarly fidelity and devastating emotional directness.

books.title: Circe
books.description: The nymph Circe, banished to a deserted island, transforms herself from minor goddess to witch of legend through exile, craft, and defiance of Olympus.

books.title: The Song of Achilles
books.description: Awkward exiled prince Patroclus loves golden Achilles from boyhood to the walls of Troy, where prophecy comes due.

authors.name: Kristin Hannah
authors.biography: Former lawyer and bestselling American author of sweeping historical fiction centered on women's endurance.

books.title: The Nightingale
books.description: Two French sisters resist the Nazi occupation in opposite ways — one sheltering a downed airman network, one surviving at home — in an ode to women's war.

books.title: The Great Alone
books.description: A volatile Vietnam POW moves his family off the grid in 1974 Alaska, where the winters are long and the greatest danger is inside the cabin.

books.title: The Four Winds
books.description: Dust Bowl Texas drives Elsa Martinelli west to California with her children, into the migrant camps and labor wars of the Depression.

authors.name: Hilary Mantel
authors.biography: English novelist who won two Booker Prizes for resurrecting Thomas Cromwell, and made the historical novel a modernist instrument.

books.title: Wolf Hall
books.description: Blacksmith's son Thomas Cromwell rises to become Henry VIII's indispensable fixer in a viscerally present-tense Tudor court.

books.title: Bring Up the Bodies
books.description: Cromwell engineers the fall of Anne Boleyn in nine months of terrifying statecraft, in the Booker-winning second act of the trilogy.

authors.name: Ken Follett
authors.biography: Welsh master of the doorstop page-turner, from wartime thrillers to cathedral-building epics spanning centuries.

books.title: The Pillars of the Earth
books.description: Builder Tom, monk Philip, and noblewoman Aliena struggle to raise a cathedral in twelfth-century England through civil war, famine, and ambition.

books.title: Eye of the Needle
books.description: A ruthless German spy carrying the secret of D-Day is stranded on a remote Scottish island with a lonely Englishwoman who must stop him.

authors.name: George Saunders
authors.biography: American short-story master of corporate dystopia and radical empathy; his first novel won the Booker Prize.

books.title: Lincoln in the Bardo
books.description: Over one 1862 night, Abraham Lincoln visits his dead son's crypt while a chorus of garrulous ghosts debate their unfinished lives.

books.title: Tenth of December
books.description: Ten stories of theme parks, pharmaceutical trials, and ordinary courage that bend dark satire toward unexpected kindness.

authors.name: Ocean Vuong
authors.biography: Vietnamese American poet and novelist whose work transmutes war inheritance, queerness, and a mother's love into incandescent language.

books.title: On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous
books.description: Little Dog writes a letter his Vietnamese mother cannot read, tracing three generations from Saigon to Hartford and his first love in tobacco fields.

authors.name: Delia Owens
authors.biography: American wildlife scientist whose debut novel, written in her late sixties, became one of the bestselling books of its decade.

books.title: Where the Crawdads Sing
books.description: Abandoned 'Marsh Girl' Kya raises herself in the North Carolina wetlands and stands trial when the town golden boy turns up dead.

authors.name: Bonnie Garmus
authors.biography: American copywriter who debuted at sixty-four with a global bestseller about a chemist who refuses to be underestimated.

books.title: Lessons in Chemistry
books.description: Sidelined 1960s chemist Elizabeth Zott becomes a subversive TV cooking-show star who teaches housewives chemistry — and self-respect.

authors.name: Gail Honeyman
authors.biography: Scottish novelist whose debut about loneliness and survival became a word-of-mouth phenomenon.

books.title: Eleanor Oliphant Is Completely Fine
books.description: Rigidly routined Eleanor's solitary life cracks open through small kindnesses, revealing the trauma her completely fine facade conceals.

authors.name: Emily Henry
authors.biography: American author whose witty, emotionally grounded romances made her the defining romance novelist of her generation.

books.title: Beach Read
books.description: A romance writer with writer's block and a literary novelist next door swap genres for a summer, and the research gets personal.

books.title: Book Lovers
books.description: Cutthroat literary agent Nora keeps colliding with brooding editor Charlie in the small town where her sister dragged her for a Hallmark-style month.

books.title: People We Meet on Vacation
books.description: Best friends Poppy and Alex take one vacation a year together until the trip that ruined everything — and the one chance to fix it.

authors.name: Nicholas Sparks
authors.biography: American author of weepy Carolina love stories, many adapted into hit films.

books.title: The Notebook
books.description: Noah reads a worn notebook to a woman in a nursing home, retelling the summer love that war, class, and now memory itself tried to erase.

authors.name: Jojo Moyes
authors.biography: English journalist turned novelist of big-hearted romantic fiction and historical drama.

books.title: Me Before You
books.description: Cheerful small-town Lou becomes caregiver to paralyzed ex-banker Will, and their six months together change both their notions of a life worth living.

authors.name: Daphne du Maurier
authors.biography: English mistress of brooding romantic suspense, whose Cornwall gothics blur the line between love story and nightmare.

books.title: Rebecca
books.description: An unnamed young bride arrives at Manderley to find her husband's first wife — the dazzling, dead Rebecca — ruling the house through the sinister Mrs. Danvers.

books.title: My Cousin Rachel
books.description: Philip is determined to hate the widow who may have poisoned his beloved cousin — until he meets her, in du Maurier's most exquisitely ambiguous novel.

authors.name: Paulo Coelho
authors.biography: Brazilian author whose fable of following one's dream became one of the bestselling books in history, translated into more than eighty languages.

books.title: The Alchemist
books.description: Andalusian shepherd Santiago journeys to the Egyptian pyramids in search of treasure and learns the universe conspires to help those who pursue their Personal Legend.

authors.name: Yann Martel
authors.biography: Spanish-born Canadian writer whose Booker-winning castaway tale asks which story of survival you prefer to believe.

books.title: Life of Pi
books.description: After a shipwreck, sixteen-year-old Pi Patel drifts across the Pacific in a lifeboat shared with a 450-pound Bengal tiger named Richard Parker.

authors.name: Markus Zusak
authors.biography: Australian novelist whose Death-narrated tale of words in wartime became a modern classic for readers of every age.

books.title: The Book Thief
books.description: In Nazi Germany, foster child Liesel steals books and shares them with neighbors and the Jewish man hidden in her basement — narrated by Death itself.

authors.name: Ruta Sepetys
authors.biography: Lithuanian American author of meticulously researched historical novels that surface buried atrocities for young adult readers.

books.title: Between Shades of Gray
books.description: Fifteen-year-old Lina is deported with her family from Lithuania to a Siberian labor camp in 1941, documenting survival through her drawings.

books.title: Salt to the Sea
books.description: Four refugees converge on the doomed Wilhelm Gustloff in 1945, in a novel of the deadliest maritime disaster in history.

authors.name: John Irving
authors.biography: American novelist of Dickensian plots, New England eccentrics, wrestling, and bears, beloved for narrative generosity.

books.title: A Prayer for Owen Meany
books.description: Tiny, shrill-voiced Owen Meany believes he is God's instrument after a foul ball kills his best friend's mother, in Irving's funny, devastating novel of faith and fate.

books.title: The World According to Garp
books.description: The life of novelist T.S. Garp, son of feminist icon Jenny Fields, unspools in a tragicomedy of lust, art, and catastrophe.

authors.name: Barbara Kingsolver
authors.biography: American novelist and biologist whose fiction binds ecology, social justice, and family with a naturalist's eye. Two-time Women's Prize winner.

books.title: The Poisonwood Bible
books.description: An evangelical missionary drags his wife and four daughters to the Belgian Congo in 1959, and Africa undoes every certainty they carried.

books.title: Demon Copperhead
books.description: David Copperfield reborn in opioid-era Appalachia: a boy with copper hair and no luck narrates foster care, football, addiction, and survival.

authors.name: Richard Powers
authors.biography: American novelist of science and conscience whose books entangle human lives with code, music, and forests.

books.title: The Overstory
books.description: Nine strangers are summoned by trees to defend a continent's last old-growth forest in a Pulitzer-winning novel that makes the arboreal world a protagonist.

authors.name: Han Kang
authors.biography: South Korean Nobel laureate whose spare, unsettling novels probe violence, refusal, and the body.

books.title: The Vegetarian
books.description: After violent dreams, Yeong-hye stops eating meat — a quiet refusal that detonates her marriage, her family, and her hold on personhood.

books.title: Human Acts
books.description: The 1980 Gwangju massacre radiates through seven linked voices, beginning with a boy searching the morgue for his friend.

authors.name: Olga Tokarczuk
authors.biography: Polish Nobel laureate of restless, constellation-built novels that cross borders of genre, time, and body.

books.title: Drive Your Plow Over the Bones of the Dead
books.description: An eccentric astrology-loving recluse in a Polish border village insists the hunters dying around her are being killed by animals taking revenge.

books.title: Flights
books.description: A constellation of stories and essays on travel, anatomy, and motion — airports, preserved hearts, a vanishing wife — that won the International Booker.

authors.name: Elena Ferrante
authors.biography: Pseudonymous Italian novelist whose Neapolitan quartet, an operatic story of a lifelong female friendship, became a global literary event.

books.title: My Brilliant Friend
books.description: Elena and Lila grow up fierce and entangled in a poor, violent Naples neighborhood, their friendship a lifelong contest and lifeline.

books.title: The Story of a New Name
books.description: Lila's brutal marriage and Elena's escape into education pull the friends apart and together through their Naples twenties.

authors.name: Ottessa Moshfegh
authors.biography: American novelist of repellent, magnetic antiheroines, praised for prose as controlled as her characters are not.

books.title: My Year of Rest and Relaxation
books.description: A beautiful, misanthropic young New Yorker decides to hibernate for a year on prescription pills, with the worst psychiatrist in literature.

authors.name: Ernest Cline
authors.biography: American screenwriter and novelist whose debut turned 1980s pop-culture obsession into a worldwide treasure hunt.

books.title: Ready Player One
books.description: In a collapsing 2045, teenage gunter Wade Watts races for a dead billionaire's Easter egg hidden inside the OASIS, the virtual world everyone lives in.

authors.name: Hanya Yanagihara
authors.biography: American novelist and magazine editor whose epic of friendship and trauma divided critics and devastated readers.

books.title: A Little Life
books.description: Four college friends rise through decades of New York success while the brilliant, broken litigator Jude guards a childhood of unspeakable harm.
